name: MHC hidden-compression prenorm GEMM (fused GEMM + per-row squared-sum)
preconditions: n_splits >= 1 (runtime scalar; the downstream consumer only ever sums across the split axis)

the split-K layout is a perf device: gemm_out_mul.sum(0) == F.linear(a,fn) and gemm_out_sqrsum.sum(0) == (a**2).sum(-1); per-split tensors are NOT element-wise equal across backends, only their sum is

numerics.notes: Outputs are fp32 (GEMM accumulation + squared-sum). Both backends upcast a (bf16->fp32) and accumulate in fp32, so divergence is fp32 accumulation order only. The mandatory sweep uses n_splits=1, where the per-split tensor equals the sum and is therefore element-wise comparable; split-K>1 keeps the sum-invariant (validated in tests/) but its per-split tensors legitimately differ and are compared via .sum(0), not element-wise.
